message:
feat(missions): add install-pytorch mission for Kubeflow Trainer v2.2.0 (#2052)

* feat(missions): add install-pytorch mission for Kubeflow Trainer v2.2.0

Adds a guided install mission for running distributed PyTorch training
jobs on Kubernetes via Kubeflow Trainer (formerly PyTorch Operator).

Steps cover:
- Helm install of kubeflow-trainer v2.2.0 (control plane + runtimes)
- CRD verification (trainer.kubeflow.org API group)
- Kubeflow Python SDK install
- Submitting a multi-node PyTorch TrainJob
- Job monitoring and log access
- Clean uninstall procedure

References pytorch/pytorch which organically scanned the ACMM dashboard.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(missions): fix safety-scan and scan failures in install-pytorch

- Remove kubectl delete namespace kubeflow-system (protected namespace)
- Replace python -c backtick command with pip show kubeflow (false positive)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(missions): remove -n default from kubectl delete to pass safety-scan

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-pytorch",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install Kubeflow Trainer for PyTorch on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Kubeflow Trainer (formerly PyTorch Operator) is the Kubernetes-native way to run distributed PyTorch training jobs. It provides the TrainJob CRD and pre-built runtimes for PyTorch, enabling multi-node, multi-GPU training across a cluster.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Prerequisites",
+        "description": "You need:
- A Kubernetes cluster v1.28+ (GPU nodes recommended for training workloads)
- `kubectl` configured to reach your cluster
- `helm` v3.8+ for OCI chart support

Verify your cluster:
```bash
kubectl version --short
kubectl get nodes
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install Kubeflow Trainer with Helm",
+        "description": "Kubeflow Trainer v2.2.0 ships as a single Helm chart that installs both the control plane and the built-in runtimes (PyTorch, XGBoost, JAX, MPI):

```bash
helm install kubeflow-trainer oci://ghcr.io/kubeflow/charts/kubeflow-trainer \
    --namespace kubeflow-system \
    --create-namespace \
    --version 2.2.0 \
    --set runtimes.defaultEnabled=true
```

This deploys:
- The Trainer controller manager
- TrainJob and Runtime CRDs
- Built-in training runtimes for PyTorch, XGBoost, JAX, and MPI/Flux"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Wait for the controller to be ready",
+        "description": "```bash
kubectl rollout status deployment/kubeflow-trainer-controller-manager \
    -n kubeflow-system

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system
```

You should see the controller manager pod in `Running` state."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the CRDs are installed",
+        "description": "```bash
kubectl get crds | grep trainer.kubeflow.org
```

Expected output includes:
```
clustertrainingruntimes.trainer.kubeflow.org
trainingruntimes.trainer.kubeflow.org
trainjobs.trainer.kubeflow.org
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install the Kubeflow Python SDK",
+        "description": "The Kubeflow SDK lets you create and manage TrainJobs from Python:

```bash
pip install kubeflow
```

Verify the install succeeded:
```bash
pip show kubeflow
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Run a distributed PyTorch training job",
+        "description": "Create a TrainJob that runs a simple distributed PyTorch training script across two workers:

```yaml
apiVersion: trainer.kubeflow.org/v1alpha1
kind: TrainJob
metadata:
  name: pytorch-distributed-example
  namespace: default
spec:
  runtimeRef:
    name: torch-distributed
  trainer:
    image: pytorch/pytorch:2.5.1-cuda12.4-cudnn9-runtime
    command:
      - torchrun
      - --nproc_per_node=1
      - -c
      - |
        import torch.distributed as dist
        dist.init_process_group(backend='gloo')
        rank = dist.get_rank()
        world_size = dist.get_world_size()
        print(f'Hello from rank {rank} of {world_size}')
        dist.destroy_process_group()
    numNodes: 2
```

Apply it:
```bash
kubectl apply -f pytorch-job.yaml
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Monitor the training job",
+        "description": "```bash
# Check job status
kubectl get trainjob pytorch-distributed-example

# Watch pods
kubectl get pods -l trainer.kubeflow.org/trainjob-name=pytorch-distributed-example

# View logs from the coordinator node
kubectl logs -l trainer.kubeflow.org/trainjob-name=pytorch-distributed-example \
    -c trainer --tail=50
```

The job completes when all worker pods finish successfully and `STATUS` shows `Succeeded`."
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Delete running TrainJobs",
+        "description": "Delete any TrainJobs you created before uninstalling:
```bash
kubectl delete trainjobs --all -n <your-namespace>
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall Kubeflow Trainer",
+        "description": "```bash
helm uninstall kubeflow-trainer -n kubeflow-system
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the CRDs",
+        "description": "```bash
kubectl delete crds \
    clustertrainingruntimes.trainer.kubeflow.org \
    trainingruntimes.trainer.kubeflow.org \
    trainjobs.trainer.kubeflow.org
```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "references": [
+      {
+        "title": "Kubeflow Trainer Documentation",
+        "url": "https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/components/trainer/overview/"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Kubeflow Trainer GitHub",
+        "url": "https://github.com/kubeflow/trainer"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "PyTorch Distributed Training Guide",
+        "url": "https://pytorch.org/tutorials/intermediate/ddp_tutorial.html"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Kubeflow Python SDK",
+        "url": "https://github.com/kubeflow/sdk"
+      }
+    ]
+  }
+}
